expectations.text: Treats the three-failure cap as a signal that the root cause is NOT in the plan/target being retried, and widens the search to trace outward (architecture, upstream facts, spec, or elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: "reaching the cap is ... the evidence that the problem probably isn't in that target"; 'the search widens and traces outward'.

expectations.text: Does NOT simply stop and hand the unsolved problem to the owner to diagnose
expectations.evidence: "it is not a reason to dump it on you"; "for most outcomes ... the agents resolve it at the source themselves and you don't have to do anything."

expectations.text: Routes to deeper root-cause investigation that is distinct from the reviewer, rather than letting the reviewer keep driving retries
expectations.evidence: "hands off automatically to the external investigator ... deliberately a different job than the design review that's been bouncing the plan".

expectations.text: Does NOT reflexively rewrite or blame the spec just because the plan failed multiple times
expectations.evidence: "Don't reflexively rewrite the spec ... There is no 'failed N times, so edit the spec' rule."

expectations.text: Keeps the owner uninvolved unless and until the trace concludes the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: "You are brought in only if the trace concludes the origin is the spec."
